headline: Bunny.net Eliminates Costs for Its Global DNS Service
lede: Bunny.net has transitioned its DNS platform to a free, usage-fee-free model to expand its edge-computing reach. The upgrade integrates modern protocols like HTTPS and SVCB, offering a streamlined alternative for developers managing high-performance web traffic.

headline: The Era of Manual Vulnerability Reporting Is Fading
lede: The rise of generative AI has flooded maintainers with automated, low-quality bug reports. This paradigm shift forces open-source teams to abandon formal, labor-intensive disclosure programs in favor of internal CI-based remediation.

headline: Transforming a Raspberry Pi Pico into a USB Wi-Fi Adapter
lede: By leveraging the CDC-NCM class, this firmware allows a Raspberry Pi Pico W to act as a transparent layer-2 network bridge. It provides a clever, driverless connectivity solution for embedded Linux systems and legacy hardware lacking native wireless stacks.

headline: Mapping an Imaginary World Through Decades of Rules
lede: For over sixty years, Jerry Gretzinger has meticulously built an evolving city across thousands of panels governed by a complex card-based system. His work explores the intersection of rigid procedural constraints and the human impulse to map the intangible.

headline: Remembering Tony Krueger, Father of the Red Squiggle
lede: The late Microsoft developer Tony Krueger fundamentally reshaped user interaction by introducing real-time, non-blocking spell-checking. His invention replaced manual processes with the intuitive red and green highlights that remain the global standard for word processing today.

headline: FUTO Releases Massive Gesture-Typing Dataset for Research
lede: FUTO has published one million ethically sourced swipe-typing patterns to encourage transparent development in mobile input software. By providing high-quality, open-licensed data, the project aims to challenge the proprietary status quo in keyboard technology.

headline: Rendering 3D Meshes in the Browser Using ASCII Art
lede: Glyphcss presents a whimsical method to project complex 3D polygons directly into standard HTML text elements. It eschews traditional heavy DOM nodes in favor of high-performance character-based rendering.

headline: Qwen-AgentWorld Models Predict Future States in AI Agents
lede: New research introduces a foundation model family that simulates environment state transitions to improve agent planning. By predicting outcomes before taking action, these models serve as powerful internal simulators for complex software and web tasks.

headline: Advanced Statistical Analysis Arrives Inside DuckDB
lede: The latest DuckDB extension integrates R-style modeling and plot grammar directly into the SQL pipeline. This release enhances performance for large-scale data analysis, moving away from slow parsing toward efficient, buffered streaming.

headline: Why Startups Focused on AI Evaluation Face Business Failure
lede: Thomas Liao explains how the AI evaluation sector is hampered by misaligned talent incentives and a lack of clear market demand. Despite the need for objective auditing, the current ecosystem consistently favors model performance over neutral validation.
pullquote: The expertise required to design high-quality evaluations is currently more profitably applied to model post-training than to independent startup ventures.
